site.title: Frederick Andersen — Keynote Speaker on AI Design Systems & Design Engineering | Figma Config 2026
site.description: Frederick Andersen is a design studio founder and keynote speaker on AI design systems, UX strategy, and design engineering. Speaking at Figma Config 2026. Available for conferences, workshops, and team sessions globally.

intro.text1: Frederick talks design through a technical, commercial, and practical lens

text2.content: He’s a {link}. He works with product teams to build critical, AI-enabled design systems.

bookPage.title: Design Engineering
bookPage.subtitle: The leadership playbook for moving your team upstream.
pitch: For years we watched good design fall apart in the handoff. By the time it shipped, the intent was gone. So we changed how we work. We taught our designers to ship production code, and we moved the whole team upstream, closer to where the real decisions get made.

form.heading: Get your free copy
form.note: Enter your email and I'll send the book straight to your inbox.
form.successText: Confirm your email and your copy of the book is on its way.
form.errorText: Something went wrong. Please try again.
form.trust: Six short chapters. No spam. Unsubscribe anytime.

quote.text: Own what the user uses. Not the file that describes it.

items.title: The one bet that works
items.description: Don't try to change the whole team at once. Pick one person. Build the right conditions around them. Then let what they become do the convincing. The exact method, step by step.

items.title: A team that works upstream
items.description: Whoever ships the code is upstream. Learn how to move designers from handing things off to making the calls that stick, now that AI has lowered the bar to code.

items.title: Work that's priced differently
items.description: Design engineering changes what you sell. Now you get measured against hiring, not against other designers. Plus the one sentence that turns the room.

bookPage.tocIntro: Six chapters. One year of real client work, distilled into a playbook you can start on Monday.

toc.title: What Design Engineering Actually Is
toc.summary: Whoever ships the code is upstream. Right now, that's not you.

toc.title: The Better Work
toc.summary: Figma tells you what it should be. Code tells you what it is.

toc.title: The Skill Stack
toc.summary: The hardest part isn't the code. It's being fine when you don't know it.

toc.title: The Operational Shift
toc.summary: I didn't transform my studio. I made one bet.

toc.title: Repositioning Your Value
toc.summary: We didn't have a pricing problem. We had a product problem.

toc.title: The Six-Month Roadmap
toc.summary: Everything before this was argument. This is the instruction.

closingCta.heading: Move your team upstream
closingCta.subheading: Get the playbook free, straight to your inbox.

bookPage.authorBio: Frederick runs EDL, a small design studio in Copenhagen. He writes and talks about design, engineering, and how teams build better products. He thinks the best work happens when the people who design it also build it.

coverImage.alt: Design Engineering: The Leadership Playbook for Moving Your Team Upstream by Frederick Andersen

aboutPage.title: About Frederick
bio: Frederick Andersen is the founder of EDL, a design studio in Copenhagen that builds AI-driven design systems for technical product teams.
He sits at the intersection of design, engineering, and product strategy — a space where most speakers don't operate. His work is grounded in real production systems, not conference-circuit theory.

Before founding EDL, Frederick worked across product design, front-end engineering, and design systems at scale. He's advised teams at startups and enterprises on how to structure design that actually ships.

Frederick speaks globally on AI design systems, UX strategy, and design engineering. He's the author of Design Engineering: The Leadership Playbook for Moving Your Team Upstream and is speaking at Figma Config 2026.
aboutPage.philosophy: Frederick's talks are built the same way he builds products: with a systems mindset, zero fluff, and a focus on what actually works in production. Every session is grounded in real work — not slides full of theory. He tailors content to the room and leaves teams with tools they can use the next day.
aboutPage.studioDescription: EDL is a design studio that builds AI-driven design systems for technical product teams. Based in Copenhagen, EDL works with startups and scale-ups to create systems that scale.
